title: Understanding the Enhanced Rate Performance of LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4 via Zn Doping in the Mn-Sites
abstract: Pristine and Zn-doped LiNi0.5Mn1.5–x ZnxO4 (x = 0, 0.01, 0.03, 0.05) cathodes were synthesized by the room-temperature solid-state method. The effects of Zn doping on crystal structure, occupation of transition metal ions in the tetrahedral Li sites, Mn valence, morphology and electrochemical performance were systematically investigated. The results indicate that moderate Mn-site Zn doping enhances cation disorder, reduces charge transfer impedance and accelerates Li+ diffusion, thus improving the <mark>rate capability</mark>. Differing from Ni-site doping, moderate Mn-site Zn doping avoids aggravated occu…

title: Enhanced Electrocatalytic Degradation of Azithromycin using an SDS-Modified PbO2 Anode: Performance, Mechanism, and Intermediate Analysis
authors: Nasrin Hadavand, Sadegh Khazalpour, Davood Nematollahi and Lida Fotouhi
abstract: The release of antibiotics into urban wastewater, driven by population growth and overconsumption, poses severe risks to ecosystems and human health due to their persistence and resistance to biodegradation. Electrochemical oxidation employing lead dioxide (PbO2) electrodes is a promising approach for degrading refractory pollutants. In this study, a βPbO2 anode was fabricated with <mark>sodium</mark> dodecyl sulfate and modified for the electrochemical degradation of the macrolide antibiotic Azithromycin in aqueous solution. The βPbO2 coating was deposited on a titanium substrate by electrodeposition in t…

title: Short Flow-Through Length in Redox Flow Battery Electrodes Enhances Performance Characteristics
authors: Valentina Menne, Kieran Oswald, Maxime van der Heijden, Tobias Greese, Timofei Solovov, Klaus Peter Zeyer and Christina Roth
abstract: This study investigates the influence of flow-through electrode length in redox flow batteries on mass transport and related changes in battery resistance and performance metrics. A modular test cell was used that accommodated electrodes between 3 to 20 cm long. Experiments were carried out at 10 and 50% state of charge and with different electrolyte velocities. Parasitic effects such as varying pumping power and inductive distortions were accounted for, enabling fair comparison of electrochemical results. <mark>Electrochemical impedance</mark> spectroscopy and pulse tests revealed that for the 20 cm elect…
